county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Highland Circuit Court (Monterey). Mountain county formed 1847 — Virginia's least populous county. Nicknamed 'Virginia's Switzerland.' Pre-1847 records in Bath and Pendleton (now WV). Highland County Historical Society has exceptional local records given small population. Strong Scots-Irish and German settlement patterns.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Virginia independent city since 1916; carved from Prince George County
county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Hopewell city records. Became independent from Prince George County 1916. Du Pont chemical plant records (WWI era). Weston Plantation. City Point — major Civil War supply depot (Grant's headquarters). Check Prince George County for pre-1916 records. Appomattox Regional Library local history.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Isle of Wight Circuit Court (Isle of Wight). One of the original 1634 shires (as Warrosquyoake, renamed 1637). Excellent colonial record survival — some of Virginia's oldest continuous county records. Bacon's Rebellion (1676) records. Isle of Wight County Museum has genealogical collections. FamilySearch has extensive LVA microfilm.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and James City Circuit Court (Williamsburg). Original 1634 shire — Jamestown (1607) is in this county. Colonial Williamsburg Foundation Research Library has unparalleled colonial records. College of William & Mary Special Collections (Swem Library). York River parish records. Bruton Parish Church registers from 1674. Archaeological records at Jamestown Rediscovery.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and King George Circuit Court (King George). Northern Neck county formed 1721 from Richmond County. Good surviving records. Dahlgren Naval base covers part of county — some community records displaced. King George County Historical Society. Potomac River families connect to Maryland records. Check Richmond County for pre-1721 entries.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and King William Circuit Court (King William). Middle Peninsula county formed 1702 from King and Queen. Good surviving records. Pamunkey and Mattaponi Indian Reservations — Virginia's oldest reservations (1646/1658 treaties). Tribal records at Virginia Indian Heritage Foundation and Smithsonian NMAI. King William Historical Society.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1828 courthouse fire; 1864 Civil War burning — nearly all records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and King and Queen Circuit Court (King and Queen Court House). 1828 fire and 1864 Civil War burning destroyed nearly all records — one of Virginia's worst-documented counties. Check New Kent for pre-1691 records. Parish registers (Stratton Major, St. Stephen's) at LVA/VHS are critical substitute sources. FamilySearch has surviving fragments.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Lancaster Circuit Court (Lancaster). Northern Neck county formed 1651. Good colonial record survival. Christ Church (1735) — Robert 'King' Carter's church, with parish registers. Mary Ball Washington Museum and Library is an excellent genealogical resource for Northern Neck families. FamilySearch has digitized LVA microfilm.

county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Lee Circuit Court (Jonesville). Virginia's westernmost county, formed 1793 from Russell and Scott. Cumberland Gap — gateway to Kentucky. Wilderness Road families documented. Lee County Historical Society. Check Russell and Washington for earlier records. Melungeon heritage in some communities — Newman's Ridge area connects to Hancock County, TN.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Virginia independent city since 1874; carved from Rockbridge County
county_metadata.research_tips: LVA and Lexington city records. Became independent from Rockbridge County 1874. Washington and Lee University Special Collections (Leyburn Library) and VMI Archives are exceptional local resources. Stonewall Jackson House. Check Rockbridge County for pre-1874 and surrounding families. Rockbridge Historical Society.
